title: Typecheck script can pass without running any type checks
reasoning: The published root package advertises a `typecheck` command, but the command only recursively asks filtered child packages to run their own `typecheck` scripts and uses `--if-present`. That means the root command can exit successfully when selected packages have no `typecheck` script, and it does not directly invoke TypeScript despite TypeScript being a dev dependency. This can make CI/release validation report success while no package was actually typechecked.
recommendation: Make the root `typecheck` script fail unless the intended TypeScript checks actually run, for example by invoking the concrete `tsc --noEmit` commands or removing `--if-present` once every package is expected to define `typecheck`.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No linked tests are included for the package script behavior, and package scripts are not validated by the shown package metadata.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a packaging or CI smoke test that runs `pnpm typecheck` and asserts the expected TypeScript projects are checked rather than silently skipped.
minimumFixScope: Update the root `typecheck` script and, if needed, add/standardize package-level `typecheck` scripts.

history.reasoning: The original evidence path still exists at package.json:27 and package.json:34, but the typecheck script has changed from the reported risky form: it is now "pnpm -r --filter './*' run typecheck" with no --if-present. Current workspace discovery shows 14 filtered child packages, and rg found each child package defines a concrete "typecheck": "tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json" script. Running pnpm typecheck exited 0 after invoking tsc --noEmit for all 14 selected packages, so the root command no longer passes without running package type checks. The root workspace itself remains excluded by the './*' filter, but the specific finding that release validation can silently skip all package typechecking is fixed in the current repository.
commands: find . -maxdepth 2 -name package.json -print | sort | wc -l && find . -maxdepth 2 -name package.json -print | sort
